title: storage-v3 #8: delete self-host guard + CLAUDE.md rewrite (closes 08cf14b)
body:
# Goal

Delete `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` and the self-host write-refusal
guard from the codebase, rewrite the CLAUDE.md sections that
recommend the sibling-working-dir pattern, and close issue
`08cf14b` (the source-repo HEAD-drift guard).

End state: jjforge has no concept of "this is the source repo,
refuse to write." The `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` env var is gone.
The `refuse_self_hosted_write` preflight is gone. CLAUDE.md
describes how jjforge mutates v3 refs without HEAD drift, so
operating in any colocated repo (including jjforge's own
source) is the default-safe story.

# Why now

Ticket 8 of 8 in the v3 storage epic (`bd98097`). The last
ticket. Depends on tickets 1–7. Once v3 mutations don't move
HEAD, the guard's reason-to-exist evaporates and the
operator-cognitive-overhead it imposes is pure cost.

Also closes the original guard issue `08cf14b`.

# Approach

1. **Delete the preflight.**
   `crates/jjf/src/preflight.rs:185–224` — remove
   `refuse_self_hosted_write` and its
   `CliError::SelfHostedWriteRefused` variant.
2. **Delete the env-var read.** Remove all
   `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` references — `crates/jjf/src/main.rs`
   and anywhere it's checked.
3. **Delete the call sites.** Every mutating verb in
   `crates/jjf/src/main.rs` invokes `refuse_self_hosted_write`
   before preflight — drop the call.
4. **Rewrite CLAUDE.md.** The section "Operating in a
   colocated jj+git repo" describes:
   - The HEAD-drift footgun (now fixed).
   - The `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` bypass (now gone).
   - The sibling-working-dir pattern (no longer needed).
   - The recovery dance (`git symbolic-ref HEAD refs/heads/main
     && git reset --hard main`).
   All of this becomes either historical or deleted. Suggested
   replacement: a 3-line note saying "jjforge v3 writes to
   `refs/jjf/*` and never moves git HEAD; no special handling
   needed for any host repo."
5. **Close `08cf14b`** with a closing comment per the
   four-section recipe: Findings (the guard delivered on its
   purpose for ~10 sessions; v3 deletes the underlying bug),
   Recommendation (delete the guard), Confidence (high),
   Open follow-ups (none).
6. **Close `bd98097`** (the v3 epic) with a comment summarizing
   the epic's outcome: design doc + 8 tickets shipped, HEAD
   drift gone, asterinas migration unblocked.

# Scope

In scope:
- Delete the preflight, env-var, and call sites.
- Rewrite CLAUDE.md's colocated-repo section.
- Close `08cf14b` and `bd98097`.
- Drop the `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST=1 ... && git symbolic-ref ...`
  recovery dance from any other docs (handoff docs, etc.).
- Post a status comment on `cc2fa96 host-asterinas-migrate`
  noting the unblock — but DO NOT re-attempt the migration in
  this ticket. That's a separate dispatch under the
  `host-asterinas` epic.

Out of scope:
- The asterinas migration itself.
- A v3 → v4 design or roadmap.
- New features layered on v3.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `grep -r JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` returns no hits in the
      code (matches in archived commit history are fine).
- [ ] `grep -r refuse_self_hosted_write` returns no hits.
- [ ] CLAUDE.md no longer describes the sibling-working-dir
      pattern as necessary; the colocated-repo section is
      either deleted or rewritten to one short positive
      statement.
- [ ] `jjf new` (or any mutating verb) runs in this very
      jjforge source repo without `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST=1`
      and without HEAD drift afterward.
- [ ] Issue `08cf14b` is closed with the four-section recipe.
- [ ] Epic `bd98097` is closed with the summary comment.
- [ ] `cc2fa96 host-asterinas-migrate` has a status comment
      noting the unblock.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` green.

# Pointers

- Code to delete: `crates/jjf/src/preflight.rs:185–224`
  (`refuse_self_hosted_write`).
- Code to scan: `crates/jjf/src/main.rs` (every mutating
  verb's preflight call).
- CLAUDE.md section to rewrite: "Operating in a colocated
  jj+git repo".
- Original guard issue: `08cf14b`.
- Design doc: `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md` ("What goes
  away").

# Out-of-scope reminder

This ticket is the celebratory delete. Do NOT add new
features here. Do NOT relitigate the v3 design. Just remove
the now-unnecessary guard and update the docs.
